@downloads.toast.downloaded.description: Title of the in-app toast that appears when Cmdr detects a new file in the Downloads folder, e.g. 'Downloaded report.pdf'. <file> wraps the filename, shown in a monospace style. The file size may be shown separately after it.

@downloads.toast.inSubdir.description: Small line under the download toast title naming the subfolder a file landed in, e.g. 'in Downloads/Chrome/'. Only shown when the file is in a subfolder of Downloads.

placeholders.subdir: the folder path relative to Downloads, with a trailing slash (e.g. Downloads/Chrome/)

downloads.toast.learnIntro: Something cool to learn about jumping to downloads

@downloads.toast.learnIntro.description: Friendly lead-in heading above the keyboard-shortcut tips in the expanded download toast. Upbeat, teaching tone.

@downloads.toast.inAppHint.description: Tip in the download toast teaching the in-app shortcut to jump to the latest download. <chip> wraps the shortcut key, shown as a styled key cap; do not translate the key glyphs themselves. 'In-app' means while Cmdr is the active app.

placeholders.key: the shortcut keys to show in the chip (e.g. ⌘J)

@downloads.toast.globalHint.description: Tip in the download toast teaching the system-wide (global) shortcut that jumps to the latest download from any app. <em> emphasizes 'any'; <chip> wraps the shortcut key as a styled key cap (don't translate the key glyphs). 'global shortcut' is the macOS term for a system-wide hotkey.

placeholders.key: the shortcut keys to show in the chip (e.g. ⌃⌥⌘J)

@downloads.toast.summaryBoth.description: One-line summary in the collapsed download toast when both shortcuts are set: 'Jump with X in-app, Y globally.' <inApp> and <global> wrap the two shortcut key caps (don't translate the key glyphs). 'in-app' = while Cmdr is active; 'globally' = from any app.

@downloads.toast.summaryInApp.description: One-line summary in the collapsed download toast when only the in-app shortcut is set. <inApp> wraps the shortcut key cap (don't translate the key glyphs). 'in-app' means while Cmdr is the active app.

@downloads.toast.summaryGlobal.description: One-line summary in the collapsed download toast when only the system-wide shortcut is set. <global> wraps the shortcut key cap (don't translate the key glyphs). 'globally' means from any app.

@downloads.toast.expandTip.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for the chevron button that expands the collapsed download toast back to its full shortcut tip. A short action phrase.

@downloads.toast.collapseTip.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for the chevron button that collapses the expanded download toast to a compact one-liner. A short action phrase.

@downloads.toast.stopShowing.description: Button on the download toast that turns these download notifications off and opens their settings. A short button label.

@downloads.toast.jumpToFile.description: Primary button on the download toast that reveals the downloaded file in Cmdr. A short button label.

downloads.notification.title: Downloaded {fileName}

@downloads.notification.title.description: Title of the macOS system notification when Cmdr detects a new download, e.g. 'Downloaded report.pdf'.

downloads.empty.message: Your Downloads folder is empty. Go there anyway?

@downloads.empty.message.description: Info toast shown when the user asks to jump to their latest download but no eligible download was found. Offers to open the Downloads folder anyway.

@downloads.empty.dismiss.description: Button on the empty-downloads toast that closes it without doing anything. A short verb.

@downloads.empty.goToDownloads.description: Primary button on the empty-downloads toast that opens the Downloads folder. 'Downloads' is the folder name; keep it capitalized.

downloads.fda.message: Cmdr needs Full Disk Access to watch your Downloads folder.

@downloads.fda.message.description: Info toast shown when jumping to the latest download fails because Cmdr lacks Full Disk Access. 'Full Disk Access' is the exact macOS permission name; keep it as-is.

@downloads.fda.dismiss.description: Button on the Full-Disk-Access download toast that closes it without doing anything. A short verb.

@downloads.fda.openSystemSettings.description: Primary button on the Full-Disk-Access download toast that opens macOS System Settings to the Full Disk Access pane. 'System Settings' is the macOS app name; keep it as-is.

downloads.warnToast.message: The {binding} shortcut jumps to your latest download from anywhere. Keep it on?

@downloads.warnToast.message.description: First-time warning toast shown the first time the user presses the global go-to-latest-download hotkey, confirming they want this system-wide shortcut active. {binding} is the shortcut keys; don't translate the key glyphs. 'from anywhere' means from any app.

placeholders.binding: the system-wide shortcut keys the user just pressed (e.g. ⌃⌥⌘J)

@downloads.warnToast.turnOff.description: Button on the global-shortcut warning toast that disables the system-wide go-to-latest-download hotkey. A short action phrase.

@downloads.warnToast.keepOn.description: Primary button on the global-shortcut warning toast that keeps the system-wide hotkey enabled. A short action phrase.

@downloads.shortcutRow.scopeTitle.description: Section heading in the Keyboard shortcuts settings for system-wide (global) shortcuts, above the go-to-latest-download row. Shown in uppercase by styling. 'global' = works from any app.

@downloads.shortcutRow.commandName.description: Name of the editable system-wide shortcut row in Keyboard shortcuts settings. <marker> wraps the '(global)' tag (shown dimmed) that signals this hotkey fires from any app. Keep the parentheses around 'global'.

@downloads.shortcutRow.modifiedTooltip.description: Tooltip on the small dot next to a shortcut that has been changed from its default binding. A short status phrase.

@downloads.shortcutRow.resetTooltip.description: Accessibility label and tooltip for the button that resets a changed shortcut to its default binding. A short action phrase.

@downloads.shortcutRow.pressKeys.description: Placeholder shown in the shortcut-editing pill while waiting for the user to press a key combination. Trailing '...' signals it's waiting; keep it.

@downloads.shortcutRow.registered.description: Status shown briefly after the system-wide shortcut was successfully claimed from macOS. A short confirmation.

@downloads.shortcutRow.notRegistered.description: Status shown when the system-wide shortcut was saved but macOS did not actually claim the hotkey (e.g. another app holds it). A short status phrase.

@downloads.shortcutRow.invalidCombo.description: Warning status shown when the chosen system-wide shortcut can't be registered because the key combination is invalid. 'combo' = key combination. Avoid the words 'error' and 'failed'.

@downloads.shortcutRow.registerFailed.description: Warning status shown when the system-wide shortcut can't be registered, with a reason from the system appended. Avoid the words 'error' and 'failed'.

placeholders.reason: a short reason describing why registration didn't work (from the system)

@downloads.shortcutRow.addModifier.description: Warning status prompting the user to include a modifier key, because a system-wide shortcut needs at least one. The glyphs in parentheses are the Command, Control, Option, and Shift keys; keep them as-is, don't translate them.

downloads.toggleDescription.bound: Press {binding} from any app to jump to your most recent download.

@downloads.toggleDescription.bound.description: Helper text under the 'Go to latest download' toggle in File system watching settings, when a system-wide shortcut is set. {binding} is the shortcut keys; don't translate the key glyphs.

downloads.toggleDescription.unbound: Jump to your most recent download from any app.

@downloads.toggleDescription.unbound.description: Helper text under the 'Go to latest download' toggle in File system watching settings, when no system-wide shortcut is set yet.
